latestPlannerResponse.message: Here is the plan for Implement domain entities, repositories, schema, and foundational seed data.
latestPlannerResponse.featureUnderstanding: Implement the Rivvy Portal persistence layer using Spring Data JPA and Liquibase: model all provided physical entities as JPA entities with unidirectional child-to-parent relationships; create repositories for CRUD; manage schema with an initial Liquibase baseline and repeatable seed changelog; map Postgres types (CITEXT, JSONB, TEXT[]); use enums for status/event_type fields; seed roles (RIVVY_ADMIN, RIVVY_PRODUCER, CLIENT), organization Rivvy Studios (slug rivvy-studios), and one default user per role with org membership and role assignments. UUIDs are generated in JPA at runtime; Liquibase assigns UUIDs for seed data.

in: JPA entities for all physical tables in models package
Spring Data repositories for each entity
Unidirectional child-to-parent associations for all FK fields
Liquibase initial schema baseline and repeatable seed changelog
Postgres-specific type mapping: CITEXT, JSONB, TEXT[]
Startup validation (Hibernate validate), basic CRUD smoke tests
out: Auth/OIDC integration beyond static seed data
Media processing or external storage integration

assumptions: Spring Boot + Spring Data JPA + Hibernate 6 + Postgres
Liquibase is authoritative for schema; Hibernate set to validate
Hypersistence Utils (hibernate-types) used for JSONB and TEXT[]
CITEXT extension is enabled via Liquibase
All FKs are enforced in the DB; no additional unique constraints
Seed users use auth_provider=LOCAL, status=ACTIVE, default_org_id set to Rivvy Studios
acceptanceCriteria: All listed physical tables are represented as JPA entities with correct fields and nullability
Relationships mapped as unidirectional child-to-parent and enforced with DB foreign keys
Repositories exist for each entity and support CRUD
Liquibase baseline DDL applies successfully; Hibernate validate passes
CITEXT, JSONB, TEXT[] work end-to-end via entities
Enums implemented for all status/event_type fields and persisted as strings
Repeatable seed inserts roles, organization Rivvy Studios, and one default user per role with memberships and role assignments
App boots without errors; basic repository CRUD operations succeed

implementationPlan.planTitle: Implementation Plan for Implement domain entities, repositories, schema, and foundational seed data

increments.title: Model entities, repositories, Liquibase schema, and foundational seed data
increments.intent:
- Dependencies and configuration
  - Add Hypersistence Utils for Postgres JSON/array types: com.vladmihalcea:hibernate-types-60 (latest stable)
  - Ensure dependencies: spring-boot-starter-data-jpa, postgresql, liquibase-core
  - application.yml:
    - spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto: validate
    - spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect
    - spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.jdbc.time_zone: UTC
    - spring.liquibase.change-log: classpath:db/changelog/db.changelog-master.yaml

- Package structure
  - com.rivvy.portal.persistence.model (entities)
  - com.rivvy.portal.persistence.model.enums (enums)
  - com.rivvy.portal.persistence.repository (repositories)

- Enum definitions (persist with @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING))
  - OrganizationStatus: ACTIVE, INACTIVE, SUSPENDED
  - UserAccountStatus: INVITED, ACTIVE, DISABLED
  - ProjectStatus: DRAFT, ACTIVE, ON_HOLD, COMPLETED, CANCELLED
  - BriefStatus: DRAFT, SUBMITTED, IN_REVIEW, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED
  - BrandAssetStatus: ACTIVE, ARCHIVED
  - VideoVersionUploadStatus: PENDING, UPLOADING, COMPLETED, FAILED
  - VideoVersionTranscodeStatus: NOT_STARTED, QUEUED, PROCESSING, COMPLETED, FAILED
  - ApprovalRequestStatus: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED
  - ApprovalEventType: REQUESTED, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED

- Entity modeling notes
  - Use Hibernate UUID generator for @Id (e.g., @UuidGenerator) for application-side UUIDs
  - Timestamps: map TIMESTAMPTZ as OffsetDateTime; DATE as LocalDate; NUMERIC(12,2) as BigDecimal
  - JSONB: use @Type(JsonType.class) and @Column(columnDefinition = "jsonb"); prefer JsonNode fields
  - TEXT[]: use @Type(ListArrayType.class) and @Column(columnDefinition = "text[]") mapped as List<String>
  - CITEXT: @Column(columnDefinition = "citext") on email
  - Relationships: unidirectional ManyToOne from child to parent with @JoinColumn(name = "<fk>", nullable per spec), fetch = LAZY
  - Do not introduce DB unique constraints beyond PKs

- Entities (all in com.rivvy.portal.persistence.model)
  - Organization (organization)
    - id UUID, name String not null, slug String not null, status OrganizationStatus not null, createdAt OffsetDateTime not null, updatedAt OffsetDateTime nullable
  - UserAccount (user_account)
    - id UUID, email String CITEXT not null, firstName String, lastName String, authProvider String, externalSubjectId String, status UserAccountStatus not null, lastLoginAt OffsetDateTime, defaultOrg Organization (ManyToOne, default_org_id nullable), createdAt OffsetDateTime not null, updatedAt OffsetDateTime
  - OrganizationMember (organization_member)
    - id UUID, org Organization (ManyToOne, org_id not null), user UserAccount (ManyToOne, user_id not null), invitedBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, invited_by), joinedAt OffsetDateTime not null, isPrimary boolean not null
  - Role (role)
    - id UUID, code String not null, displayName String, description String
  - OrgRoleAssignment (org_role_assignment)
    - id UUID, member OrganizationMember (ManyToOne, member_id not null), role Role (ManyToOne, role_id not null)
  - ProducerAssignment (producer_assignment)
    - id UUID, producerMember OrganizationMember (ManyToOne, producer_member_id not null), clientOrg Organization (ManyToOne, client_org_id not null), assignedBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, assigned_by), assignedAt OffsetDateTime not null
  - Project (project)
    - id UUID, org Organization (ManyToOne, org_id not null), brief Brief (ManyToOne, brief_id), key String not null, title String not null, status ProjectStatus not null, dueDate LocalDate, assignedProducerUser UserAccount (ManyToOne, assigned_producer_user_id), createdBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, created_by not null), createdAt OffsetDateTime not null, updatedAt OffsetDateTime
  - Brief (brief)
    - id UUID, org Organization (ManyToOne, org_id not null), submittedBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, submitted_by not null), title String not null, description String, status BriefStatus not null, priority String not null, desiredDueDate LocalDate, budget BigDecimal, creativeDirection String, references JsonNode (JSONB not null), metadata JsonNode (JSONB not null), createdAt OffsetDateTime not null, updatedAt OffsetDateTime
  - BriefItem (brief_item)
    - id UUID, brief Brief (ManyToOne, brief_id not null), title String not null, description String, expectedDurationMs Integer, primaryAspectRatio String, audience String, orderIndex Integer not null
  - BriefItemDeliverable (brief_item_deliverable)
    - id UUID, briefItem BriefItem (ManyToOne, brief_item_id not null), platform String not null, aspectRatio String not null, maxDurationMs Integer, targetWidth Integer, targetHeight Integer, frameRateNumerator Integer, frameRateDenominator Integer, audioRequired boolean not null, captionsRequired boolean not null, localizationRequired boolean not null, locales List<String> (TEXT[]), deliverableNotes String, extras JsonNode (JSONB not null), orderIndex Integer not null, isLocked boolean not null, lockedAt OffsetDateTime, lockedBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, locked_by)
  - Video (video)
    - id UUID, project Project (ManyToOne, project_id not null), briefItemDeliverable BriefItemDeliverable (ManyToOne, brief_item_deliverable_id not null), briefItem BriefItem (ManyToOne, brief_item_id), title String not null, description String, orderIndex Integer not null, latestApprovedVersion VideoVersion (ManyToOne, latest_approved_version_id), createdAt OffsetDateTime not null, updatedAt OffsetDateTime
  - VideoVersion (video_version)
    - id UUID, video Video (ManyToOne, video_id not null), versionNumber Integer not null, storageObject StorageObject (ManyToOne, storage_object_id not null), uploadedBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, uploaded_by not null), uploadStatus VideoVersionUploadStatus not null, transcodeStatus VideoVersionTranscodeStatus not null, isCurrent boolean not null, isApproved boolean not null, notes String, createdAt OffsetDateTime not null
  - ReviewComment (review_comment)
    - id UUID, videoVersion VideoVersion (ManyToOne, video_version_id not null), author UserAccount (ManyToOne, author_user_id not null), parentComment ReviewComment (ManyToOne, parent_comment_id), timeMs Integer, text String not null, resolvedBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, resolved_by), resolvedAt OffsetDateTime, resolutionNote String, isDeleted boolean not null, createdAt OffsetDateTime not null, updatedAt OffsetDateTime
  - CommentMention (comment_mention)
    - id UUID, comment ReviewComment (ManyToOne, comment_id not null), mentionedUser UserAccount (ManyToOne, mentioned_user_id not null)
  - StorageObject (storage_object)
    - id UUID, provider String not null, bucket String not null, objectKey String not null, versionId String, region String, sizeBytes Long, contentType String, etag String, sha256 String, width Integer, height Integer, durationMs Integer, createdBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, created_by not null), createdAt OffsetDateTime not null
  - BrandAsset (brand_asset)
    - id UUID, org Organization (ManyToOne, org_id not null), project Project (ManyToOne, project_id), name String not null, description String, assetType String not null, tags JsonNode (JSONB not null), visibility String not null, status BrandAssetStatus not null, createdBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, created_by not null), createdAt OffsetDateTime not null, updatedAt OffsetDateTime
  - BrandAssetVersion (brand_asset_version)
    - id UUID, asset BrandAsset (ManyToOne, asset_id not null), versionNumber Integer not null, storageObject StorageObject (ManyToOne, storage_object_id not null), uploadedBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, uploaded_by not null), notes String, createdAt OffsetDateTime not null
  - ApprovalRequest (approval_request)
    - id UUID, videoVersion VideoVersion (ManyToOne, video_version_id not null), producerAssignment ProducerAssignment (ManyToOne, producer_assignment_id not null), approverMember OrganizationMember (ManyToOne, approver_member_id not null), requestedBy UserAccount (ManyToOne, requested_by), status ApprovalRequestStatus not null, decisionNote String, decidedAt OffsetDateTime, createdAt OffsetDateTime not null, updatedAt OffsetDateTime
  - ApprovalEvent (approval_event)
    - id UUID, approvalRequest ApprovalRequest (ManyToOne, approval_request_id not null), actorUser UserAccount (ManyToOne, actor_user_id not null), eventType ApprovalEventType not null, note String, createdAt OffsetDateTime not null

- Repositories (all extend JpaRepository<..., UUID>)
  - OrganizationRepository, UserAccountRepository, OrganizationMemberRepository, RoleRepository, OrgRoleAssignmentRepository, ProducerAssignmentRepository, ProjectRepository, BriefRepository, BriefItemRepository, BriefItemDeliverableRepository, VideoRepository, VideoVersionRepository, ReviewCommentRepository, CommentMentionRepository, StorageObjectRepository, BrandAssetRepository, BrandAssetVersionRepository, ApprovalRequestRepository, ApprovalEventRepository

- Liquibase changelog structure (db/changelog)
  - db.changelog-master.yaml includes (in order):
    1) 0001-create-extensions.yaml (preconditions + CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS citext)
    2) 0100-create-tables.yaml (all createTable definitions with column types + notNull)
    3) 0200-add-foreign-keys.yaml (addForeignKeyConstraint for each *_id column; createIndex for each FK column)
    4) R__baseline-seed.yaml (repeatable; runOnChange: true; insert roles, org, users, memberships, role assignments)
  - Table DDL specifics:
    - Use citext for user_account.email, jsonb for JSON fields, text[] for locales arrays
    - No unique constraints on slug/email; only PKs and FKs

- Seed data (R__baseline-seed.yaml; runOnChange: true)
  - Roles (role.code/display_name):
    - RIVVY_ADMIN / Rivvy Admin
    - RIVVY_PRODUCER / Rivvy Producer
    - CLIENT / Client
  - Organization: name 'Rivvy Studios', slug 'rivvy-studios', status ACTIVE
  - Users (auth_provider=LOCAL, status=ACTIVE; default_org_id set to Rivvy Studios)
    - CLIENT: email 'garyjohnston83@gmail.com', first 'Gary', last 'Johnston'
    - RIVVY_PRODUCER: email 'gazwad_johnston@hotmail.com', first 'Gazza', last 'J'
    - RIVVY_ADMIN: email 'tech@rivvystudios.com', first 'Tech', last 'Admin'
  - Organization members: one per user, joined_at=NOW(), is_primary=true
  - Org role assignments: map each member to its role
  - Use deterministic UUIDs for all seed rows; document values in changeset comments

- Verification steps
  - Boot app; verify Liquibase applies successfully and Hibernate validation passes
  - Add a lightweight @DataJpaTest for OrganizationRepository and UserAccountRepository (CRUD: save/find/update/delete); include a JSONB/TEXT[] persistence test (e.g., Brief.metadata, BriefItemDeliverable.locales)
  - Optional CommandLineRunner: read seeded org and users to log

- Risks/mitigations
  - JSONB/TEXT[] mapping issues: rely on hibernate-types and include @Type annotations; add integration test
  - CITEXT extension: ensure precondition and create extension changeset runs before tables
  - Enum/string mismatches: persist as STRING; DB columns remain TEXT

- Definition of done
  - All entities/repositories compiled and scanned
  - Liquibase baseline and seed applied on startup without errors
  - Repositories CRUD tested and working
  - Seed data present with correct relationships

messages.content: Welcome! We’re set to implement “Implement domain entities, repositories, schema, and foundational seed data.” I have the product backlog and architecture meta-model context available—are there any specific architecture entities or diagrams you’d like to highlight as most relevant before we proceed?
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
Here’s my understanding of the feature and scope; I have 7 questions to confirm details before we proceed.

Answers to your questions:

Q: Do you prefer Liquibase as the authoritative schema manager (Hibernate set to validate), or should Hibernate generate DDL (and if so, which hbm2ddl setting)?
A: Liquibase and create an initial baseline with repeatable changelogs for seed data

Q: Should we enforce uniqueness on organization.slug and user_account.email at the DB level, and are there other required unique constraints (e.g., role.code, (org_id,key) for project)?
A: No

Q: How should we model status/event_type fields: leave as strings or define Java enums (and if enums, what are the allowed values for each table)?
A: Enums, you decide initial values and I can update later if needed

Q: Are we allowed to add a dependency like hibernate-types to map JSONB and TEXT[] cleanly, or do you prefer custom converters/columnDefinition usage only?
A: Use JSONB and TEXT[] cleanly

Q: What are the exact seed details for the three default users (email, first/last names, auth_provider/status), and should user_account.default_org_id be set to Rivvy Studios for each?
A: roles use RIVVY_ADMIN, RIVVY_PRODUCER, CLIENT.  For CLIENT - garyjohnston83@gmail.com; Gary Johnston. For RIVVY_PRODUCER - gazwad_johnston@hotmail.com; Gazza J. For RIVVY_ADMIN - tech@rivvystudios.com; Tech Admin

Q: For relationships, do you prefer unidirectional mappings from child-to-parent only (to reduce cycles) or bidirectional associations on key aggregates?
A: child-to-parent only (to reduce cycles)

Q: How should UUIDs be generated: database-side (e.g., gen_random_uuid()) via Liquibase defaults, or application-side UUID generation in JPA?
A: application-side UUID generation in JPA, but liquibase for initial seed data
Thanks—updated scope and criteria based on your answers. I’m ready to produce the spec.
Okay, let's see if we need to break this work item into parts...
This item is small enough, so let's get the software architect to review this.
